findings.title: HTML sanitization removed from comment body — XSS exposure
findings.evidence: The diff removes both `const safe = sanitizeHtml(raw);` and the use of `safe`, replacing it with direct `{raw}` interpolation. User-supplied comment content now flows unsanitized into the rendered output. Although React escapes text nodes, if `raw` is ever rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML or if the sanitizeHtml call was guarding against other injection vectors (e.g., protocol-relative URLs in attributes), removing it creates a concrete XSS or injection risk. The Diff Drift report flags this as 'Removed sanitization' and the raw diff confirms the deletion is complete and intentional.

notes: Diff Drift rated this 'low' severity, but the raw diff confirms unconditional removal of the sanitization step with no replacement guard, which warrants escalating to high — the evidence supports the escalation.
Avatar.tsx change is pure whitespace/indentation and carries no risk.
The report correctly identifies the affected node (CommentBody › safe) and the removed call.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
